rag_rules: Fix unresolved property references by ensuring all property definitions and constants are properly declared in the AADL model or its dependencies before use in AGREE contracts.
Resolve type mismatches in AGREE expressions by aligning helper declarations with the actual types of referenced features or values.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure all identifiers and types referenced in expressions are visible and properly declared in the same annex or available through configured dependencies.
Use only visible AADL features or declared auxiliary variables in AGREE guarantees and assumptions to avoid 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors.
Validate that binary expressions in AGREE use operands of compatible types (int, real, bool) to prevent type mismatch diagnostics.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE expressions must have consistent types; use eq declarations to define intermediate boolean expressions for comparisons.
AGREE const and eq declarations require valid scalar types: int, real, or bool.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new property definitions or constants in AGREE annexes that are not already declared in the AADL model or its dependencies.
Avoid placing guarantees in implementation scopes; use assign for output bindings instead.
